channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Study Links Chili Pepper Consumption to Increased Cancer Risk, But Researchers Caution Against Overinterpretation
item.description: A recent study suggests a potential link between chili pepper consumption and an increased risk of certain cancers, though researchers emphasize the need for caution in interpreting the findings. The ...

item.title: Ukraine Govt Reshuffle: Zelensky Reorganizes Foreign Policy Amid War Challenges
item.description: Ukraine’s Prime Minister, Yulia Svyrydenko, has resigned as President Volodymyr Zelensky initiates a major government reshuffle to reposition the country amid escalating military, diplomatic, and econ...

item.title: BBC Licence Fee Rise Sparks Drop in Payments as Half a Million Stop Paying
item.description: The BBC has faced growing concerns over its financial sustainability following a significant rise in the annual licence fee, which now stands at £180. Recent data reveals that half a million people ha...

item.title: NASA Advances Lunar Exploration with Four New Missions to Support Permanent Moon Base
item.description: NASA has announced the selection of four critical lunar missions as part of its broader strategy to establish a sustainable human presence on the Moon. These missions, funded by a $600 million investm...

item.title: China Expands Visa-Free Entry to 50+ Countries, Excludes Most African Nations
item.description: China has expanded its visa-free entry policies to allow citizens of over 50 countries to visit for tourism or business without prior visas, effective July 2026. The country operates four distinct vis...

item.title: Brighton Signs Croatia Defender Luka Vuskovic in Record £46m Deal
item.description: Brighton Football Club has finalized the signing of young Croatian defender Luka Vuskovic from Tottenham Hotspur in a club-record transfer worth £46 million. The 19-year-old centre-back, who is set to...

item.title: Man arrested for alleged murder of girlfriend
item.description: A man named Richard Emmanuel was arrested by police in Rivers State after allegedly killing his girlfriend, Caroline Sunday, following a heated argument in the Eneka area. The police spokesperson, Ble...

item.title: Cape Town arrests 420 suspects in car theft crackdown following chaotic collision
item.description: Four alleged car thieves inadvertently foiled their own getaway after crashing into each other in two stolen vehicles in Khayelitsha, leading to their arrest as part of a citywide crime crackdown. The...

item.title: Phenylephrine in Cold Tablets May Not Relieve Nasal Congestion
item.description: A popular ingredient in cold and flu tablets, phenylephrine, has been found to have little to no effect when taken orally, despite being widely sold. Research shows it does not meaningfully relieve na...

item.title: Supreme Court rejects stay on CBSE's three-language policy, cites language learning benefits
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has rejected a plea to stay the Central Board of Secondary Education's (CBSE) three-language policy for the 2026-27 academic session. The court emphasized that 'learning a l...

item.title: Celebrity couple Aryna Sabalenka and Georgios Frangulis enjoy romantic getaway to Mykonos after Wimbledon
item.description: Aryna Sabalenka, the world No. 1 tennis player, was spotted enjoying a relaxed post-Wimbledon holiday in Mykonos with her fiancé, Georgios Frangulis. The couple, who have been dating since 2021, were ...

item.title: Andy Burnham's Political Ascent and the Uncertain Path Ahead
item.description: The article discusses the impending leadership transition in the UK Labour Party, with Andy Burnham set to become prime minister following Keir Starmer's resignation. It critiques the lack of formal c...

item.title: LGBT Communities Embrace Nationalist Politics in Europe
item.description: The article explores the growing alignment between European LGBT communities and conservative anti-immigration political parties, arguing that this shift reflects a pragmatic response to global attitu...

item.title: Filipino Women Lead Southeast Asia in Adopting Gemini AI, Reports Google
item.description: A recent report by Google highlights that Filipino women (Pinays) are at the forefront of adopting Gemini AI technology across Southeast Asia. The study underscores the growing digital literacy and te...

item.title: Bangkok music bar fire death toll rises to 30, dozens hospitalized
item.description: A devastating fire at a Bangkok music bar on Sunday night has claimed 30 lives, with over 70 people injured, according to officials. The Rong Beer Na Ladprao bar, located in the northern part of the T...

item.title: France and Spain clash in World Cup semi-final amid racism row over former PM's comments
item.description: France and Spain face off in a high-stakes FIFA World Cup semi-final, with the match overshadowed by a controversy over a former Spanish prime minister's column that sparked accusations of racism. The...

item.title: Trump Allies Seek to Remove Interventionist Republicans After Graham's Death
item.description: The article discusses how right-wing hardliners within the Republican Party are leveraging the sudden death of Senator Lindsey Graham to eliminate the GOP's remaining interventionist faction and conso...

item.title: WHO warns of critical funding gap for DRC Ebola response
item.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) is warning that it has only secured about 40% of the $115 million needed to combat the Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo. This funding s...

item.title: Karnataka High Court Condemns 12-Year POCSO Trial Delay, Calls Adjournment Culture 'Silent Accomplice'
item.description: The Karnataka High Court has strongly criticized the 'culture of repeated adjournments' in criminal trials, highlighting a 12-year delay in the POCSO (Protection of Children from Sexual Offences) tria...

item.title: Exercise Redefines Heart Nerve Networks, Offering New Treatment Insights
item.description: A groundbreaking study published in Autonomic Neuroscience reveals that regular aerobic exercise remodels the heart's nerve network in a left-right asymmetric manner. Researchers from the University o...
